project.name: coreX AI MindLayer
project.description: Universal .ai.json-based standard for assistant-readable architecture and AI-ready project knowledge. This repository is the meta-tool that creates, validates and maintains AIM files for other projects.

red_lines: Keep AIM a compass, not a chronicle.
Never compromise context reduction with redundant historical noise.
Keep the core lightweight, modular and schema-valid.
Do not auto-write project memory without clear user consent or an explicit command.
Preserve user-authored documentation and avoid destructive cleanup without review.
Validate .ai.json, examples, schemas and docs before releases.
Do not put secrets, tokens, passwords or private keys into AIM files.
When changing schemas, update examples, validators, VS Code extension schemas, docs and changelog together.

workflow.development: Use the VS Code extension development host for interactive testing and run focused npm validation scripts before pushing.
workflow.distribution: Use npm scripts and VSIX packaging for extension builds; keep package.json, package-lock.json, CHANGELOG.md and .ai.json project.version in sync.
workflow.validation: Run npm run validate:example for the public example and npm run validate -- .ai.json for the repository AIM. CI also checks JSON syntax, PHP syntax and integration smoke paths.
workflow.schema_policy: Root .ai.json follows the v4 Root Compass schema. Feature-level details should live in .ai.features.index.json and .ai.features.*.details.json instead of bloating the root file.
release_checklist: Package and test VS Code extension when extension code changed

architecture.core: Root validation and scanning logic for AIM files, including validate.js, ai-init.php and ecosystem scanners.
architecture.schemas: JSON schemas live under vscode-extension/src/schemas and are consumed by CLI validation and the VS Code extension.
architecture.commands: VS Code commands initialize projects, validate workspaces, copy context, add detail files, install agent anchors and prepare AI onboarding.
architecture.views: VS Code Tree View surfaces project identity, red lines, architecture and feature areas from AIM data.
architecture.ecosystem: Composer, PHP scanner, npm and Docker integration helpers live under scripts/ecosystem.
architecture.ci: GitHub Actions validate example AIM syntax/schema, PHP syntax, ai-init.php and integration smoke paths.

user_preferences.response_format: ausführlich bei Architekturentscheidungen, kompakt bei reinen Statusmeldungen

workflow_optimizations.title: Dokumentations-Analyse-Workflow
workflow_optimizations.summary: AIM first, then README/CHANGELOG and only then deeper files. This avoids blind searching and keeps project context coherent.

workflow_optimizations.title: Complete Read First
workflow_optimizations.summary: For documentation or schema work, read the relevant complete files before editing to avoid hidden dependency and redundancy issues.

workflow_optimizations.title: AJV schemas need stable local keys when no $id is present
workflow_optimizations.summary: validate.js registers schemas with explicit local keys like ai.schema.json to avoid duplicate empty AJV schema IDs.
